current_blocker: Project_ARIS clean clone still contains an orphan 160000 gitlink at aris-active-context while .gitmodules is absent and git submodule status exits 128.

repair_plans_evaluated.reason: A documentation-only replacement is too weak for the current repository shape because BOOT.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, tests, and tracked artifacts still reference the aris-active-context path directly. Removing the gitlink without introducing a single explicit replacement anchor would leave a diffuse contract and increase follow-up churn.

A dedicated pointer file can replace the orphan gitlink with one explicit external-source contract, while a narrow set of root docs and tests are updated to reference that file instead of a hidden nested checkout. This is the smallest coherent patch that preserves the external canonical-source model and avoids recreating .gitmodules.

No primary evidence shows any historical .gitmodules contract or a legitimate need for a live submodule. Reintroducing .gitmodules would widen scope and contradict the accepted triage bias toward an external canonical pointer.
The read-only evidence is already sufficient to choose a narrow repair direction without mutating Project_ARIS yet.

selected_repair_plan_rationale: The repository already treats aris-active-context as a pointer, not as a healthy submodule, and multiple commits describe updates to it as pointer syncs.

Tracked root guidance files such as BOOT.md, CLAUDE.md, and AGENTS.md still instruct readers to consume files under the aris-active-context path; that path will disappear once the orphan gitlink is removed.

A single replacement file such as Project_ARIS/ACTIVE_CONTEXT_POINTER.md provides a stable textual contract that can declare MatheusAugDEV/aris-active-context as the canonical external source and spell out the new read-first access rule.
This keeps the future patch small: remove the gitlink from the index, add the pointer file, and update only the minimal set of root docs/tests that currently rely on the nested path contract.
The plan remains fail-closed because baseline execution stays blocked until the gitlink is removed, the pointer contract is committed, and validation confirms there is no orphan submodule state left.

rejected_repair_plans.rejection_reason: Too underspecified for the number of existing nested-path references.
No evidence of a real submodule contract.
Evidence already supports a conservative plan selection.

future_project_aris_patch_scope.summary: Future repair removes the orphan gitlink entry aris-active-context, adds one explicit external-context pointer file, and updates only the smallest set of tracked docs/tests needed to route readers and validators to the external canonical repository.
patch_sequence: Record the current gitlink SHA a113ad549f0db6c4cb9d8c4aead7d654eb3938da in the repair artifact and pointer file for audit continuity.
Remove the orphan gitlink from the Project_ARIS index and working tree contract without touching the external aris-active-context repository.
Add ACTIVE_CONTEXT_POINTER.md that names MatheusAugDEV/aris-active-context as the canonical external source and explains the read-first contract outside the Project_ARIS tree.
Rewrite the minimal root docs/tests that still hard-code aris-active-context/* local paths so they reference ACTIVE_CONTEXT_POINTER.md and the external repository contract.
Run targeted validations that prove no orphan submodule state remains before any baseline test is attempted.

future_validation_matrix.expected_result: Pointer file states that MatheusAugDEV/aris-active-context is the canonical external active-context source and explains read-first usage.

future_validation_matrix.command: git grep -n 'aris-active-context/CURRENT_STATE.md\|aris-active-context/NEXT_ACTION.md\|aris-active-context/BOOT_PROFILE.md\|aris-active-context/READ_PROFILE.md' -- BOOT.md CLAUDE.md AGENTS.md tests/test_f21a54c_active_context_remote_sync_verification.py || true
future_validation_matrix.expected_result: Removed or intentionally replaced by pointer-file references in the planned minimal targets.
Run only after V1-V6 pass and the orphan gitlink blocker is fully resolved.

rollback_strategy.safe_rollback_point: Before the future Project_ARIS repair commit.
rollback_strategy.rollback_method: If the future repair introduces regressions, revert the single Project_ARIS repair commit and restore the prior clean clone from remote rather than reconstructing the orphan gitlink manually.
notes: Rollback must not touch the quarantined dirty trees.
Rollback does not require or authorize recreating .gitmodules.
If the pointer-file patch fails before commit, discard only the clean clone workspace and reclone from the canonical remote.

conditions: The orphan gitlink has been removed from the Project_ARIS index.
ACTIVE_CONTEXT_POINTER.md exists and documents the external canonical repository.
Minimal root docs/tests selected in the repair no longer depend on the removed nested path contract.

artifact_sha256.self_hash_limitation: The terminal file sha256 is reported externally after write because a file cannot embed its own final sha256 without self-reference drift.
